itineraries.name: Three-day Tokaj village circuit: Tokaj town, Mád and Tarcal
itineraries.theme: Tokaj Hegyalja foundations: historic town, top-teroir village and estate landscapes

days.morning: Arrive in Tokaj town and walk down to the Tisza-Bodrog confluence for the panoramic view of the Hegyalja hills. Visit the Tokaj Museum on Bethlen Gábor utca for the history of Aszú production before the first tasting appointment.
days.lunch: Lunch at a riverside restaurant in Tokaj town with a glass of local Szamorodni száraz alongside Tisza fish dishes.
days.afternoon: Enter the Rákóczi Cellar beneath Tokaj town for the candlelit heritage cellar walk and Aszú tasting. The cellar tunnels run for several hundred metres and maintain a constant cool temperature for Aszú maturation.
days.evening: Dinner in Tokaj town followed by an evening tasting at one of the cellar bars in the main square.
days.accommodation: Hotel in Tokaj town or guesthouse at Tarcal.

days.morning: Drive 15 km west to Mád for a morning appointment tasting at one of the village's leading estates. Mád's single-vineyard Furmint producers including Holdvölgy, Barta Pince and Szepsy are the focus of the visit.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Mád village restaurant with a Furmint pairing alongside traditional Hungarian fish soup or stuffed cabbage.
days.afternoon: Walk the Nyulászó and Betsek vineyard rows above the village in the afternoon; vineyard walking maps are available at Holdvölgy and Royal Tokaji Mád locations. Optional second tasting appointment at a second Mád producer in the late afternoon.
days.evening: Return to Tarcal or stay in Mád for the evening; the village has limited accommodation but the night quietude and stargazing above the vineyard is exceptional.

days.morning: Morning visit to the Disznókő estate at Tarcal for the full cellar tour and tasting, including the underground barrel cellar and the panoramic terrace above the Lapis vineyard.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Disznókő estate restaurant or terrace with the estate's dry and sweet range poured with food pairings.
days.afternoon: Drive north to Erdőbénye or Tolcsva for a final afternoon appointment at a smaller estate, then loop back south via the Bodrog valley road to Tokaj for the return journey to Budapest.
days.evening: Train or car return to Budapest, arriving by 21:00.

itineraries.description: Three-day circuit covering the Tokaj Hegyalja's three key reference points: the historic wine-trade town of Tokaj, the single-vineyard terroir village of Mád, and the AXA-owned Disznókő estate at Tarcal. Combines heritage cellar visits, vineyard walks and producer tastings across dry Furmint and Aszú.
itineraries.tip: Book Disznókő two to three weeks in advance; Mád producers including Holdvölgy take bookings one to two weeks ahead. The Rákóczi Cellar requires no advance booking.

itineraries.name: Weekend Aszú deep dive: Royal Tokaji, Disznókő and Oremus
itineraries.theme: Tokaji Aszú across three leading estates: comparing the three founding Western-reinvestment houses

days.morning: Morning appointment at Royal Tokaji in Mád for the full Aszú vertical tasting, including the estate's single-vineyard Nyulászó and Szt. Tamás Aszú bottlings alongside the Late Harvest and dry Furmint.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Royal Tokaji Mád visitor reception or at the village restaurant before the afternoon visit.
days.afternoon: Drive 15 km east to Tarcal for an afternoon appointment at Disznókő. The cellar tour covers the barrel cellar, the Lapis vineyard parcels and a comparative vertical of Aszú across recent vintages.
days.evening: Dinner in Tarcal or Tokaj town; Szamorodni száraz with fish as the pre-dinner aperitif.
days.accommodation: Guesthouse at Tarcal or hotel in Tokaj town.

days.morning: Drive 30 km south along the Bodrog valley to Tolcsva for a morning appointment at Oremus, the Vega Sicilia-owned estate. The tasting focuses on the estate's Mandolás dry Furmint, Szamorodni and the Aszú range.
days.lunch: Lunch at Oremus estate or at one of the Tolcsva village restaurants overlooking the Bodrog plain.
days.afternoon: Return north along the Bodrog valley road stopping at the Bodrogkisfalud and Bodrogolaszi riverbank viewpoints before joining the motorway south to Budapest.

itineraries.description: Two-day Aszú-focused weekend visiting the three flagship Western-reinvestment estates: Royal Tokaji (Hugh Johnson, 1990), Disznókő (AXA, 1992) and Oremus (Vega Sicilia, 1993). A comparative tasting across the three key cellar styles of post-communist Tokaj revival.
itineraries.tip: All three estates require advance booking; Royal Tokaji and Disznókő are accessible on 7-day lead time, Oremus on 7-14 days. The vertical Aszú tastings across all three in one weekend give the most comprehensive view of how the three reinvestment estates interpreted the Aszú tradition differently.

itineraries.name: Mád single-vineyard pilgrimage: Szepsy, Holdvölgy and Barta
itineraries.theme: Mád terroir focus: comparing single-vineyard dry Furmint across three boutique estates

days.morning: Begin with an appointment tasting at Szepsy István's cellar in Mád for the single-vineyard dry Furmints (Betsek, Úrágya and Nyulászó) alongside the Aszú bottlings. This is the originating cellar of the dry Furmint renaissance.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Mád village restaurant or at a small gastro-café in the village centre with traditional Zemplén cuisine.
days.afternoon: Walk the Nyulászó and Betsek vineyard rows above the village before a 15:00 appointment at Holdvölgy for a comparative single-vineyard Furmint tasting from their Mád parcels. Optional third appointment at Barta Pince for the Öreg Király and Vén Király single-vineyard bottlings if time allows.
days.evening: Return to Budapest or overnight stay in Mád.
days.accommodation: Guesthouse in Mád or hotel in Tokaj town.

itineraries.description: Single-day Mád village pilgrimage comparing the dry Furmint approach of three boutique estates in the top vineyard sites. Szepsy, Holdvölgy and Barta are the three Mád producers whose single-vineyard Furmints most clearly articulate the terroir differences between the Nyulászó, Betsek and Király sites.
itineraries.tip: Szepsy appointments are the hardest to secure; contact three to four months in advance. Holdvölgy and Barta can typically accommodate bookings on 7-14 days' notice.

itineraries.name: Budapest to Tokaj weekend by train
itineraries.theme: Car-free Tokaj weekend: train travel + walking distance cellar visits near Tokaj town station

days.morning: Take the morning Intercity from Budapest Keleti station to Tokaj (approximately 2 hours 30 minutes). On arrival, check in at the hotel and walk to the Tisza-Bodrog confluence and the Tokaj Museum.
days.lunch: Lunch at a riverside restaurant overlooking the Bodrog; fish soup with dry Furmint by the glass.
days.afternoon: Afternoon candlelit tasting at the Rákóczi Cellar beneath Tokaj town, followed by a walk through the historic town centre and up to the Tokaj hill viewpoint overlooking the vineyards.
days.evening: Dinner in Tokaj town with the local Hétszőlő Furmint and Tokaj Trade House Aszú paired with traditional Hungarian dinner dishes.

days.morning: Hire a local taxi or bicycle to reach Tarcal (3 km from Tokaj town) for a morning appointment at Disznókő. The estate has a well-structured visitor programme.
days.lunch: Lunch at Disznókő estate or return to Tokaj town for lunch before the return journey.
days.afternoon: Return to Tokaj town station and catch the afternoon Intercity train back to Budapest Keleti.

itineraries.description: Car-free weekend taking the Budapest Keleti to Tokaj Intercity train, staying in Tokaj town, visiting the Rákóczi Cellar heritage site and the Disznókő estate at Tarcal on a bicycle or taxi hop. Accessible to visitors without a car and covers the historic core of the region in two days.
itineraries.tip: Intercity tickets must be reserved in advance on the MÁV website; the direct Budapest Keleti to Tokaj service is the most convenient rail option. Bicycle hire in Tokaj town is available at the tourist information office on the main square.

itineraries.name: Harvest week: Aszú berry-picking experience
itineraries.theme: Aszú harvest participation: joining the October berry-picking and cellar fermentation at two estates

days.morning: Arrive in Mád and check into accommodation. Orientation visit to the first estate (Royal Tokaji Mád) for a harvest briefing, covering the Aszú berry selection criteria and the day-by-day progress of botrytis in the vineyards.
days.lunch: Harvest lunch at the Royal Tokaji estate with seasonal Zemplén food.
days.afternoon: First Aszú berry-picking session in the Nyulászó vineyard; pickers select individual shrivelled botrytised grapes by hand. An hour of picking typically yields a small puttony basket.
days.evening: Estate dinner with the harvest team; fresh grape must and previous vintage Aszú poured alongside traditional harvest food.

days.morning: Second morning picking session in the vineyard; continue Aszú berry selection with the estate team. The picking typically runs from 08:00 to 13:00 before the afternoon heat makes the grapes less firm.
days.afternoon: Cellar observation session: watch the Aszú paste preparation and the maceration of the botrytised berries in Furmint base wine. The winemaker leads the technical explanation.

days.morning: Travel to Tarcal for two nights at Disznókő. Morning vineyard walk through the Lapis parcel above the estate to observe the botrytis progression on the Furmint clusters.
days.afternoon: Aszú berry-picking session at Disznókő; the estate uses large estate-wide teams and visitors join a single picking row. Followed by a barrel-cellar walk to see the previous vintage Aszú in cask.

days.morning: Rest morning or optional visit to Tokaj town for the museum and Rákóczi Cellar tasting.
days.afternoon: Afternoon Aszú tasting vertical at Disznókő cellar: five vintages of Aszú 5 puttonyos to compare the harvest character year by year.
days.evening: Farewell harvest dinner at a Tarcal restaurant with local Zemplén cuisine and a bottle of Disznókő Aszú.

days.morning: Morning drive or train to Budapest; the harvest season leaves the Hegyalja valley particularly golden in morning light. Stop at a roadside fruit stand for the seasonal Zemplén plums.
days.afternoon: Optional Tokaj wine shopping at Bortársaság Budapest before the journey home.

itineraries.description: Five-day harvest-week experience combining Aszú berry-picking at Royal Tokaji and Disznókő, with cellar observation sessions, vertical Aszú tastings and harvest dinners. The most immersive possible engagement with the Tokaj wine cycle.
itineraries.tip: Both Royal Tokaji and Disznókő run harvest visitor programmes but spaces are very limited and fill months in advance; contact estates in May or June for October harvest programme availability.
